review_summary: The transcript is sufficient for concept questions on I3D weight inflation and the distinction between temporal action localization and full spatio-temporal detection.

question_bank.question: Why does I3D initialize an inflated 3D kernel by copying a pretrained 2D kernel Kt times and dividing by Kt?
choices: To reuse image-pretrained weights while keeping the response sensible for constant-frame videos
To force the model to ignore temporal information completely
To reduce the number of output channels to one
To replace fine-tuning with a closed-form solution
question_bank.explanation: This initialization transfers useful image features into a video model. Dividing by Kt keeps the overall response scale reasonable, and a constant video behaves consistently with the original 2D filter.

question_bank.question: What is the difference between temporal action localization and spatio-temporal detection?
choices: Temporal localization finds when an action happens, while spatio-temporal detection finds both when and where it happens
Temporal localization classifies clips, while spatio-temporal detection estimates optical flow
Temporal localization uses audio only, while spatio-temporal detection uses RGB only
There is no difference; the terms mean the same thing
question_bank.explanation: Temporal localization focuses on the time interval of an action. Spatio-temporal detection adds spatial localization, so the system must also identify where the action occurs in the frames.
